text: When designing a REST API, it is important to follow best practices to make it reliable and user-friendly. Start by creating clear and predictable endpoints. For example, use nouns like `/users` or `/orders` instead of verbs. Always use appropriate status codes to show the result of a request, such as 200 for success, 404 for not found, or 500 for server errors. Good documentation is essential, as it helps developers understand how to use your API effectively. Finally, consider versioning your API, for instance by using `/v1/` in the URL. This allows you to make changes without breaking existing clients. Following these guidelines makes your API easier to work with.
